_meta.method: Web research on major Chan/Zen institutions with HK presence: Plum Village (AIAB), Dharma Drum HK, Chung Tai HK.
_meta.coverageEstimate: High for institutional Chan/Zen practice centers. Lower for smaller lay sanghas.
knownGaps: Smaller Plum Village lay sanghas in Hong Kong not enumerated.
Fo Guang Shan HK branches may exist but not separately verified.

places.name: Asian Institute of Applied Buddhism (AIAB) – Lotus Pond Temple
places.lineage: Plum Village / Vietnamese Thien (Thich Nhat Hanh)
places.address: Lotus Pond Temple, Ngong Ping, Lantau Island, Hong Kong
places.notes: Plum Village monastic practice center for Asia, founded by Thich Nhat Hanh; resident monks and nuns lead retreats.

places.name: Dharma Drum Mountain Hong Kong Centre
places.address: Room 205, 2/F, Block B, Alexandra Industrial Building, 23-27 Wing Hong Street, Lai Chi Kok, Kowloon, Hong Kong
places.notes: Hong Kong branch of Dharma Drum Mountain; offers regular Chan meditation classes and Sheng-yen teachings.

places.name: Pu Guang Meditation Center (Chung Tai HK)
places.address: G/F, Kingstown Mansion, 319-323 Jaffe Rd., Wanchai, Hong Kong
places.notes: Hong Kong branch meditation center of Chung Tai Chan Monastery.

places.name: Pu Men Temple Hong Kong (Foguangshan)
places.address: 23 Sik On Street, Wan Chai, Hong Kong
places.notes: Foguangshans main branch in Hong Kong; offers public Chan practice and Sunday classes.

places.name: Po Lin Monastery
places.address: Ngong Ping, Lantau Island, Hong Kong
places.notes: Founded 1906; one of Hong Kongs most important Buddhist monasteries. Tian Tan Buddha is on its grounds; long-standing Chan training site.

places.name: Chi Lin Nunnery
places.address: 5 Chi Lin Drive, Diamond Hill, Kowloon, Hong Kong
places.notes: Founded 1934, rebuilt 1990s in Tang-dynasty wooden architecture. Active Chan-affiliated nunnery in central Kowloon.
